channel.title: Posts on Erykor's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Erykor's Blog

item.title: Fear of Success
item.description: I don’t realize success is unconsciously perceived as a threat by myself until recently. The concept of fearing success may seem counterintuitive. After all, isn’t success what we all strive for? However, ‘achievemephobia’, or the fear of success, is a genuine psychological condition that prevents many individuals from reaching their full potential. Those afflicted may subconsciously sabotage their own efforts, creating self-imposed barriers to their success. This fear is often rooted in past experiences, learned behaviors, and societal conditioning, making it a complex issue to navigate.

item.title: Why We Feel a Problem Is Hard
item.description: When we perceive a problem as difficult, this hardness can be attributed to various factors. It’s essential to demystify the concept and develop a more precise and shared understanding of it. Here are some factors that contribute to the difficulty of a problem: Hidden and implicit information: Critical information required for solving the problem may be missing, and you need to identify what that information is. This often requires adopting a different perspective from your normal view.
